revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}}{{complex}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} risings as the birth hour of the {{Ill|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{Ill|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|en|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis-Festival=Sothis-Festival|lt=Sopdet festival}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten}} * {{Ill|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]]

The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten}} * {{Ill|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}}{{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}}{{complex}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} risings as the birth hour of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|en|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis-Festival=Sothis-Festival|lt=Sopdet festival}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:11]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]]

It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} risings as the birth hour of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|en|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis-Festival=Sothis-Festival|lt=Sopdet festival}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} risings as the birth hour of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|12=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|12=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=draft}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]]

''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}}{{complex}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=draft|12=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=draft|12=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=draft|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=draft|12=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|12=draft|12=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]]

''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}}{{complex}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=draft|12=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=draft|12=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=draft|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=draft|12=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|12=draft|12=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]]

''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}}{{complex}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=draft|12=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=draft|12=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=draft|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=draft|12=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|12=draft|12=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). ''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}}{{complex}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]]

Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}}{{complex}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]]

''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}}{{complex}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). revision.comment: 25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten) up to rev 245510179
revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Zwölfte Nachtstunde |NAME = N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht'' |NAME2 = p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut'' |BILD1 = Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde }} Die '''zwölfte Nachtstunde''' in der [[Dämmerung|Morgendämmerung]] bezeichnete im [[Altes Ägypten|Alten Ägypten]] einerseits die letzte Stunde der Nacht und andererseits die letzte Stunde des [[Tag (Altes Ägypten)|altägyptischen Tages]]. Sie wurde durch den [[Sonnenaufgang]], der die erste Tagesstunde im [[Ägyptischer Kalender|altägyptischen Kalender]] markierte, beendet. [[Ägyptische Mythologie|Mythologisch]] stand die zwölfte Nachtstunde seit dem [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reich]] unter der Schirmherrschaft von [[Isis]] und [[Sopdet]], Göttinnen über das Licht und die [[Keku-semau|Finsternis]]. Die zwölfte Nachtstunde [[symbol]]isierte insbesondere die Zeit der [[heliakisch]]en [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekan-Aufgänge]] als Geburtsstunde der [[Baktiu]]: {{Zitat|Herauskommen aus der [[Duat]], sich niederlassen in der Morgenbarke. Den [[Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|Nun]] befahren zur Stunde des Re. Sich verwandeln in [[Chepre|Chepri]] und Aufsteigen zum Horizont. Eintreten in den Mund, Herauskommen aus der [[Vulva]]. Aufleuchten in der Türöffnung des Horizontes zur Stunde, die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren schaut, um den Lebensbedarf der Menschen zu schaffen, des Viehs und allen Gewürms, das Re erschaffen hat.|[[Buch der Nacht]]}} In der zwölften Nachtstunde und damit der letzten Stunde des alten Jahres, am fünften Tag der [[Heriu-renpet]], erfolgte außerdem der heliakische Aufgang des [[Sirius]], [[Stern]] der Göttin Sopdet. Mit dem kurz danach beginnenden Sonnenaufgang schloss sich die Neujahrsfeier des [[Sothis-Fest|Sopdet-Festes]] für den [[Tag (Altes Ägypten)|ersten Tag]] des Jahres an. Seit der [[Spätzeit (Ägypten)|Spätzeit]] hatte die Göttin [[Nebet-seschep]] als ''Herrin des Lichts'' die Funktionen von Isis und Sopdet übernommen. Aus den [[Inschrift]]en des [[Naos der Dekaden]] geht hervor, dass die [[Astrologie|astrologische]] Wirksamkeit des jeweiligen Dekansternbildes einer Dekade mit der heliakischen [[Kulmination (Astronomie)|Kulmination]] in der zwölften Nachtstunde beginnt. == Siehe auch == * [[Stundengottheiten]] * [[Unut|Wenut]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren.'' Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne. Das sogenannte Nutbuch'' (= ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications.'' Band 31 / ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Band 8). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies u. a., Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. {{SORTIERUNG:Zwolfte Nachtstunde}} [[Kategorie:Ägyptischer Kalender (Altertum)]] [[Kategorie:Astronomie im Alten Ägypten]] [[Kategorie:Geschichte der Zeitmessung]]

''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut'' |BILD1 = Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde }} Die '''zwölfte Nachtstunde''' in der [[Dämmerung|Morgendämmerung]] bezeichnete im [[Altes Ägypten|Alten Ägypten]] einerseits die letzte Stunde der Nacht und andererseits die letzte Stunde des [[Tag (Altes Ägypten)|altägyptischen Tages]]. Sie wurde durch den [[Sonnenaufgang]], der die erste Tagesstunde im [[Ägyptischer Kalender|altägyptischen Kalender]] markierte, beendet. [[Ägyptische Mythologie|Mythologisch]] stand die zwölfte Nachtstunde seit dem [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reich]] unter der Schirmherrschaft von [[Isis]] und [[Sopdet]], Göttinnen über das Licht und die [[Keku-semau|Finsternis]]. Die zwölfte Nachtstunde [[symbol]]isierte insbesondere die Zeit der [[heliakisch]]en [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekan-Aufgänge]] als Geburtsstunde der [[Baktiu]]: {{Zitat|Herauskommen aus der [[Duat]], sich niederlassen in der Morgenbarke. Den [[Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|Nun]] befahren zur Stunde des Re. Sich verwandeln in [[Chepre|Chepri]] und Aufsteigen zum Horizont. Eintreten in den Mund, Herauskommen aus der [[Vulva]]. Aufleuchten in der Türöffnung des Horizontes zur Stunde, die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren schaut, um den Lebensbedarf der Menschen zu schaffen, des Viehs und allen Gewürms, das Re erschaffen hat.|[[Buch der Nacht]]}} In der zwölften Nachtstunde und damit der letzten Stunde des alten Jahres, am fünften Tag der [[Heriu-renpet]], erfolgte außerdem der heliakische Aufgang des [[Sirius]], [[Stern]] der Göttin Sopdet. Mit dem kurz danach beginnenden Sonnenaufgang schloss sich die Neujahrsfeier des [[Sothis-Fest|Sopdet-Festes]] für den [[Tag (Altes Ägypten)|ersten Tag]] des Jahres an. Seit der [[Spätzeit (Ägypten)|Spätzeit]] hatte die Göttin [[Nebet-seschep]] als ''Herrin des Lichts'' die Funktionen von Isis und Sopdet übernommen. Aus den [[Inschrift]]en des [[Naos der Dekaden]] geht hervor, dass die [[Astrologie|astrologische]] Wirksamkeit des jeweiligen Dekansternbildes einer Dekade mit der heliakischen [[Kulmination (Astronomie)|Kulmination]] in der zwölften Nachtstunde beginnt. == Siehe auch == * [[Stundengottheiten]] * [[Unut|Wenut]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren.'' Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne. Das sogenannte Nutbuch'' (= ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications.'' Band 31 / ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Band 8). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies u. a., Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. {{SORTIERUNG:Zwolfte Nachtstunde}} [[Kategorie:Ägyptischer Kalender (Altertum)]] [[Kategorie:Astronomie im Alten Ägypten]] [[Kategorie:Geschichte der Zeitmessung]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}}{{complex}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|qq=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}}

It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|qq=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}}

{{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}}{{complex}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|qq=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]]

It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|qq=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]]

''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decan|lt=decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|en|lt_original=Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}} risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|de}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the {{ill|Nu (Egyptian mythology)|lt=Nun|en||de|Nun (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft}} at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the {{ill|Naos of the Decades|de|Naos der Dekaden|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Naos of the Decades|qq=draft}} show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]]

''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Hours]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] }} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[en:draft:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]]
