revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] }} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomie im Alten Ägypten]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]]

Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Ancient Egyptian astronomy
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] }} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]]

The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] }} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]]

''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[:en:Isis|Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]]

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} . It was ended by [[sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[:en:Isis|Isis]] and [[Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[:en:Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[:en:Vulva|Vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[Sirius]], [[:en:Star|Star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]

Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]], [[:en:Star|Star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]

''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[:en:Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} . It was ended by [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[:en:Isis|Isis]] and [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[:en:Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[:en:Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[:en:Vulva|Vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]], [[:en:Star|Star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[:en:Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} . It was ended by [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[:en:Isis|Isis]] and [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[:en:Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[:en:Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[:en:Vulva|Vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]], [[:en:Star|Star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[sn:Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). {{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[:en:Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} . It was ended by [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[:en:Isis|Isis]] and [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[:en:Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[:en:Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[:en:Vulva|Vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]], [[:en:Star|Star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

[[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] {{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[:en:Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} . It was ended by [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[:en:Isis|Isis]] and [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[:en:Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[:en:Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[:en:Vulva|Vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]], [[:en:Star|Star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=|WD=Q2303813}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=|WD=Q2359142}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] {{wikidata link|Q245250}}

Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=|WD=Q2359142}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=|WD=Q1464360}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). revision.text:
[[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] {{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[:en:Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} . It was ended by [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[:en:Isis|Isis]] and [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[:en:Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[:en:Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[:en:Vulva|Vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]], [[:en:Star|Star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=|WD=Q2303813}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=|WD=Q2359142}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=|WD=Q1464360}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] {{wikidata link|Q245250}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

[[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] {{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[:en:Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} . It was ended by [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[:en:Isis|Isis]] and [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[:en:Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[:en:Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[:en:Vulva|Vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]], [[:en:Star|Star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=|WD=Q2303813}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=|WD=Q2359142}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=|WD=Q1464360}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] {{wikidata link|Q245250}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes

''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[:en:Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} . It was ended by [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[:en:Isis|Isis]] and [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[:en:Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[:en:Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[:en:Vulva|Vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]], [[:en:Star|Star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=|WD=Q2303813}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=|WD=Q2359142}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=|WD=Q1464360}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] {{wikidata link|Q245250}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q245250}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=|WD=Q2303813}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=|WD=Q2359142}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=|WD=Q1464360}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] {{wikidata link|Q245250}}

revision.text:
[[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] {{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[:en:Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} . It was ended by [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[:en:Isis|Isis]] and [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[:en:Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[:en:Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[:en:Vulva|Vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]], [[:en:Star|Star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=|WD=Q2303813}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=|WD=Q2359142}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=|WD=Q1464360}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] {{wikidata link|Q245250}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] {{wikidata link|Q245250}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "pages with conflicting qids" to "qq"
revision.text:
[[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] {{afc comment|1=enwiki might already cover the hours here in [[Amduat]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:47, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Zwölfte Nachtstunde|name=N14-X1:Z1-mD:Z1*Z1-N35:N2|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=Wenut-medj-senu-en-gereh
''Wnwt-mḏ-snw-n-grḥ''
''Zwölfte Stunde der Nacht''|name2=p*t:r-M7-D4*D4:nfr*nfr*nfr-nb-A40:R23|NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION=Peteret-neferu-nebes
''Ptrt-nfrw-nbs''
''Die die Vollkommenheit ihres Herren sieht/schaut''|BILD1=Twilight3 - NOAA.jpg|BILD1-BREITE=280px|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG=Morgendämmerung als zwölfte Nachtstunde}} In [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egypt,]] the '''twelfth hour of the night''' [[:en:Twilight|at dawn]] marked both the last hour of the night and the last hour of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=ancient Egyptian day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} . It was ended by [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]], which marked the first hour of day in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|ancient Egyptian calendar]] . [[Religion of ancient Egypt|Mythologically,]] since the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom,]] the twelfth hour of the night was under the patronage of [[:en:Isis|Isis]] and [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]], goddesses of light and {{ill|Keku-semau|lt=darkness|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qq=}} . The twelfth hour of the night [[:en:Symbol|symbolized]] in particular the time of the [[:en:Heliacal rising|heliacal]] [[:en:Decan|decan]] risings as the birth hour of the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} :{{Blockquote|Emerging from the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], settling in the morning barque. Sailing the [[:en:Nu (mythology)|Nun]] at the hour of Re. Transforming into [[:en:Khepri|Khepri]] and ascending to the horizon. Entering the mouth, emerging from the [[:en:Vulva|Vulva]]. Lighting up in the doorway of the horizon at the hour that beholds the perfection of its lord, to create the sustenance of humans, of livestock, and all creatures that Re has made.|{{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch der Nacht|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}}}In the twelfth hour of the night and thus the last hour of the old year, on the fifth day of {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}}, the heliacal rising of [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]], [[:en:Star|Star]] of the goddess Sopdet, also occurred. As the sun rose shortly afterwards, the New Year celebration of the {{ill|Sothis Festival|en|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|Sothis Festival=Sothis Festival|lt=Sopdet festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|qq=|WD=Q2303813}} for the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|lt=first day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=|WD=Q1669420}} of the year began. Since the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|lt=Late Period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|qq=}}[[:de:Spätzeit_des_Alten_Ägyptens|,]] the goddess [[Nebet-seschep]] had taken over the functions of Isis and Sopdet as ''mistress of light'' . The inscriptions of the [[:en:Naos of the Decades|Naos of the Decades]] show that the [[:en:Astrology|astrological]] effectiveness of the respective decan constellation of a decade begins with the heliacal {{ill|culmination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/culmination|qq=}} in the twelfth hour of the night. == See also == * {{ill|Hour deities|de|Stundengottheiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hour deities|qq=|WD=Q2359142}} * {{ill|Unut|en|4=de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unut|qq=|WD=Q1464360}} == literature == * [[:de:Christian_Leitz|Christian Leitz]] : ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks'' . Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 9-0683-1669-9 * [[:de:Alexandra_von_Lieven|Alexandra von Lieven]] : ''Outline of the course of the stars.'' ''The so-called groove book.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies and a., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 ( ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute Publications'' 31), ( ''The Carlsberg Papyri'' 8). {{translated page|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|version=245510179|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)]] {{wikidata link|Q245250}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
